ai_research.headline: Claude Mythos triggers ASL-4 lockdown — UK banks getting access anyway — Terafab chip fabs race to 'light speed'

items.title: Claude Mythos: first 10T+ param model, ASL-4 locked, UK banks getting access
items.body: Anthropic's Claude Mythos (10T+ params, 800B-1.2T active per MoE pass, trained on 15.5T tokens) triggered ASL-4 safety protocol, blocking public/API release. Despite warnings from finance leaders and ministers about cybersecurity risks, UK banks gain access next week. Scored 83.1% on CyberGym benchmarks — the cybersecurity expert clusters are the headline capability. US banks already have access; ECB warning European bankers separately.

items.title: Terafab: $25B Musk chip fab moving at 'light speed' in Austin
items.body: Tesla-SpaceX Terafab joint venture targeting 2nm chips for FSD, Robotaxi, Optimus, xAI, and orbital data centers. Suppliers (Applied Materials, Tokyo Electron, Lam Research, Samsung) getting holiday weekend quote requests with premium payment offers. Pilot line: 3K wafers/month, full production by 2029, aiming for 1 terawatt compute capacity. Intel already signed on.

items.title: Q1 AI funding hit $297B — 81% of all venture went to AI
items.body: Monthly recap: Q1 2026 saw $297B in AI funding (81% of total VC). Agentic AI now mainstream with 43% of enterprises in production. OpenAI's $110B raise (Amazon-led) and SpaceX-xAI merger ($250B, largest M&A ever) dominate the headlines.

ai_research.learn_today: Claude Mythos is the first model to trigger ASL-4 (Anthropic's highest safety tier). The fact that banks are getting access despite the lockdown means Anthropic is selectively deploying its most dangerous model to controlled environments. This is the 'AI safety in practice' story of the year.

openclaw_agents.headline: Agentic AI hits 43% enterprise adoption — Mythos cybersecurity clusters could reshape agent capabilities

items.title: 43% of enterprises now have agentic AI in production
items.body: Q1 2026 data shows agentic AI crossed the enterprise adoption tipping point. The shift from chatbots to autonomous agents doing real work (coding, research, operations) is no longer experimental. 57% running agents in production per LangChain's report last week.
items.try_this: If you're building with agents, the production reliability gap is where the opportunity is. Lifecycle callbacks, observability, and cost control are the unsolved problems.

items.title: Claude Mythos cybersecurity expert clusters — what agents could become
items.body: Mythos has specialized expert clusters for cybersecurity (full attack chain analysis), academic research, and complex software engineering. The CyberGym benchmark score (83.1%) suggests it can autonomously find and exploit vulnerabilities. This is what 'agent' means at the frontier — not a chatbot, but a specialist that can own an entire workflow.

openclaw_agents.try_today: It's Friday. The SpaceX IPO is shaping up to be the biggest in history ($75B raise, $1.75-2T valuation). NASDAQ bending rules to make it happen. If you have any exposure to space/defense/AI stocks, worth paying attention to the timeline.

tesla.note: Holding near $390 after yesterday's AI5 tape-out surge. Terafab ramping aggressively — 2nm chip production targeting 2029. Q1 earnings next Tuesday (April 22). Stock essentially flat today after big yesterday.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: Confidential NASDAQ IPO filed April 1. Targeting $1.75-2T valuation, $75B raise (largest ever). NASDAQ waiving 10% free float requirement and shortening seasoning to 15 days for index inclusion. Alphabet's 6.11% stake could be worth $120B+. History warns mega-IPOs average -10% at 6 months. Starlink driving 50-80% of revenue.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Part of the Terafab ecosystem now — Musk's chip empire spans Tesla (FSD/Robotaxi), SpaceX (orbital data centers), xAI (models), and Optimus (robots). No standalone xAI news today, but the infrastructure buildout is the story.

items.title: Dust Bunny (2026)
items.note: Mads Mikkelsen plays a hitman hired by a girl to kill a giant dust bunny under her bed. Sigourney Weaver co-stars. Bryan Fuller directing. This sounds unhinged in the best way.

items.title: Roommates (2026)
items.note: Sadie Sandler, Chloe East, Natasha Lyonne. College freshman with a boundary-free roommate. Comedy that could go either way — but the cast is solid.

items.title: A Gorilla Story: Told by David Attenborough
items.note: Attenborough reflecting on gorilla Pablo's life 50 years later. If you like nature docs, Attenborough narrating is always worth your time.

items.title: Margo's Got Money Troubles
items.note: Elle Fanning as a single mom turning to OnlyFans. Dropped April 15 — still fresh. Apple TV+ dramedies have been strong lately.

items.title: Friday is QuizzyDots promo day — growth post went out yesterday
items.body: Growth post id=94 went out yesterday (Tax Day reward angle). Friday's 'brain dump' post scheduled for 2:30 PM ET. Monitor engagement over the weekend — Friday afternoon posts often get picked up over the weekend when people have time to browse.

items.title: Mythos cybersecurity angle for InstaClaw positioning
items.body: Claude Mythos just proved AI can find zero-days autonomously. If InstaClaw has any security/monitoring features, the narrative window is wide open: 'AI that defends your computer, not just chats about it.' This is the kind of frontier capability story that makes local AI feel relevant.

macro.mood: Markets quiet on Good Friday (markets closed). TSLA flat after yesterday's surge. LMND continues uptrend at $68. BTC pushing $75.5K. All eyes on SpaceX IPO timeline and TSLA earnings Tuesday.
macro.btc_friday_note: Markets closed today (Good Friday). Prices are Wednesday's close.
